sections.paragraph:
fut. יִדַּח prop. to thrust (ftoffen), to impel. (see the cogn. דָּחָה and the observations made there); hence-

(1) to thrust forth, to expel, 2Sa 14:14 see Hiphil.

(2) to thrust against, (as an axe against a tree), followed by עַלּ Deu 20:19.

Niphal נִדַּח-

(1) pass. of Kal No. 2. to be thrust forth, to be impelled; Deu 19:5, “if he go with his neighbour into a wood to cut timber וְנִדְּחָה יָדֹו בַגַּרְזֶן לִכְרֹת הָעֵץ וְנו׳ and his hand is impelled with the axe,” i.e. lifts up the axe to cut down a tree.

(2) pass. of Hiphil No. 2, to be expelled, driven out, Jer 40:12 whence part. נִדָּח one expelled, an outcast, Isa 16:3, Isa 16:4 27:13 . Collect. Deu 30:4 Neh 1:9, and fem. נִדָּחָה Mic 4:6 Zep 3:19, outcasts. With suff. נִדְּחֹו driven away by him, 2Sa 14:13.-Figuratively, Job 6:13, תּוּשִׁיָּה נִדְּחָה מִמֶּנִּי “health has fled away from me.” Used of dispersed and wandering cattle, Deu 22:1 Eze 34:4, 16 Eze 34:16.

(3) pass. of Hiphil No. 3, to be seduced, to suffer oneself to be seduced, Deu 4:19 30:17 .

Pual, to be driven onward, Isa 8:22, אֲפֵלָה מְנֻדָּח “driven to darkness,” compare Jer 23:12.

Hiphil הִדִּיחַ-

(1) to thrust down, to cast down, Psa 5:11, followed by מִן Psa 62:5.

(2) to thrust out, to expel, to drive away, 2Ch 13:9, e.g. the Israelites into other countries, Deu 30:1 Jer 8:3 23:3 , Jer 23:8 29:14 , 18 Jer 29:18 32:37 46:28 to scatter a flock, Jer 23:2 50:17 .

(3) to seduce any One, Deu 13:14 Pro 7:21 followed by מִן to draw away from any thing, Deu 13:6 מֵעַל יְחֹוָה verse 11 Deu 13:11.

(4) to bring, to draw down (evil) on any one, followed by עַל 2Sa 15:14, compare Kal No. 2.

Hophal, part. מֻדָּח driven up and down, Isa 13:14.

Derivative noun, מַדּוּחִים.
